extraction.one_line_summary: ABERASTURI outsprinted BYSTRØM from a late breakaway that held off the peloton by seconds in the final 1.5km
tactical_patterns: ABERASTURI deliberately sat on BYSTRØM's wheel knowing the Norwegian was stronger sprinter, attacking only at 200m on uphill finish
VALVERDE forced YATES Simon to chase personally in final 2.5km, preventing GC leader from conserving energy before summit stages
Breakaway alliance collapsed under 1.5km when riders looked at each other, nearly gifting sprinters the catch despite 23-second gap

rider_signals.ABERASTURI Jon: positive: tactically mature, deliberately parasited stronger sprinter BYSTRØM then timed 200m attack on uphill gradient perfectly
rider_signals.BYSTRØM Sven Erik: negative: stronger sprinter but opened sprint too early, unable to hold off ABERASTURI's late acceleration on gradient
rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: forced to personally mark VALVERDE in closing kilometers of sprint stage, revealing GC anxiety about gaps
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: created positioning pressure forcing race leader YATES to chase personally rather than relying on team
rider_signals.FELLINE Fabio: negative: exhausted himself leading out sprinters in doomed chase, mistimed team effort
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: neutral: fully committed to sprint despite late breakaway, shows willingness to gamble on marginal catches
